_notes: Custom dialogue script for Specialist Kovacs at LC-4 Relay.
mood_change: positive = improves mood, negative = sours mood, 0 = no change.
response_pool: Kovacs picks one randomly. Use {name} for farm name placeholder.
unlocks: list of topic IDs this response makes available. Strict limit of ONE id per object to match singular engine injection.
tier: 1 = always available, 2 = unlocked by a tier 1 topic baseline or progressive tier 2 chains.
visible_label: what the player sees as the button text.
mood_hint: optional subtle hint shown AFTER the player has used this option 3+ times.

topics.visible_label: How are you doing, Kovacs?
topics.mood_hint: He appreciates basic professionalism.
response_pool: Surviving. Just another shift in the tin can.
Managing. Data traffic is heavy today.
Could be worse. I could be stationed on an unpressurized cargo rock.
Still here. The hull hasn't breached yet, so I suppose that is a positive.
Stuck at the desk. Another cycle, another thousand shipping manifests to route.
I am functional. The main comm matrix is stable, even if my patience is not.

topics.visible_label: Anything good in the cafeteria today?
topics.mood_hint: He is highly particular about his daily routines.
response_pool: Terrible. They ran out of the good coffee beans again. It is synthetic sludge today.
The automated beverage dispensers are acting up. I cannot even get a clean brew.
The food is standard ration blocks, but the coffee situation is an absolute tragedy.
They subbed the morning protein blocks with generic brand paste. It is a dark day.
Nutritious but completely devoid of joy. And the espresso unit is completely down for maintenance.
Do not ask. If I have to drink one more cup of this dehydrated chicory blend, I am filing a protest.

topics.visible_label: How did a guy with your crisp uniform end up routing manifests?
topics.mood_hint: This clearly touches a heavy nerve about his career.
response_pool: Because the assignment committee does not look at merit. I am incredibly overqualified.
A lot of station politics, frankly. I am meant for a bridge, not a switchboard.
Every appeal I file gets buried in bureaucracy. Someone with my training should be running a crew.
Seniority management bottlenecks. I aced the command track, yet here I sit sorting logistics.
Let us just say my talents are currently frozen in sub-optimal placement infrastructure.
The board claims operational necessity. I claim they do not know how to read an exam profile.

topics.visible_label: You look exceptionally well-prepared for a standard shift.
topics.mood_hint: He prides himself on maintaining officer standards.
response_pool: Thank you. Standards don't drop just because you are in a tin can. That is character. It keeps me sane, along with my routines.
I appreciate that. It is a matter of discipline. I keep my station, my uniform, and my small personal quarters in perfect order.
Someone has to maintain professional standards out here. If you let your routine slip, the isolation wins.
A proper officer represents the fleet regardless of posture or placement. I refuse to get sloppy.
Thank you. The logistics crew down on deck three have completely abandoned their grooming protocols. It is disgusting.
Discipline is the only defense against deep-space rot. I press this fabric every single morning.

topics.visible_label: Have the communication lines been quiet today?
topics.mood_hint: He loves monitoring and judging the chatter of other sectors.
response_pool: Quiet? No. The channels are flooded with absolute amateurs messing up basic procedures.
It is mostly a stream of surface operators failing to balance their atmospheric grids.
I have spent half my shift logging standard protocol infractions from greenhorn farmers.
The local sub-space bands are chaotic today. Nobody seems to understand standard audio etiquette.
Just a lot of chatter from people who shouldn't have been granted an operator license.
It is exhausting. Every honyocker in the quadrant is hitting the emergency transponder for minor soil issues.

topics.visible_label: Is the background rumble on the station always this loud?
topics.mood_hint: He hates the physical reality of his desk location.
response_pool: The primary ventilation scrubber for deck four is right behind my partition. It is constant noise.
Environmental controls refuse to damp the low-frequency vibrations. My teeth rattle during peak hours.
It is a constant mechanical drone. If I had my own command bridge, acoustic dampening would be standard.
The deck plating vibrates whenever a heavy freighter docks. It drives me absolutely mad.

topics.visible_label: Is the artificial gravity working normally today?
topics.mood_hint: He spends a lot of time monitoring basic station metrics.
response_pool: It is holding steady at zero-point-nine-eight gees. It fluctuates whenever the main capacitors are cycling.
Currently stable. Though the engineers were messing with the magnetic dampeners on deck two earlier.
Stable enough. Though I occasionally feel a slight micro-gravity drift in the mornings.

topics.visible_label: Are there any interesting supply ships docking today?
topics.mood_hint: He closely tracks all transit lanes.
response_pool: Nothing but heavy ore freighters and bulk agricultural haulers. It is entirely tedious.
Just the standard fleet logistics transport. They brought in a fresh batch of raw minerals.
The arrival boards are full of corporate haulage. No military or exploration vessels, unfortunately.

topics.visible_label: Let us skip the pleasantries and open the trade lines.
topics.mood_hint: He detests being treated like a machine.
response_pool: Right. Professional. Fine.
Always a pleasure. Let us just process the data.
Straight to business then. Fine by me.
Manifest open. Let us speed this up.
Acknowledged. I will skip the status update then.
Very well. Processing surface requests without further commentary.

topics.visible_label: Have the ventilation registers been dusted recently?
topics.mood_hint: He has no strong feelings about standard maintenance.
response_pool: They are scheduled for every second Tuesday of the quarter. Nothing exciting.
Standard maintenance handles that. I do not monitor dust levels.
A sterile environment is standard. They are as clean as they need to be.

topics.visible_label: Is your terminal screen brightness comfortable?
topics.mood_hint: Display preferences do not affect his operational status.
response_pool: It is at seventy percent. Standard office regulation for twelve-hour shifts.
The auto-dimmer is functional. I have no complaints regarding display illumination.
Contrast is fine. Just standard amber text on black.

topics.visible_label: Does the station run on fleet standard time?
topics.mood_hint: Chronological alignment is a standard background metric.
response_pool: Coordinated Universal Time. It aligns with the primary colony servers.
Station cycle is synchronized with base command. No adjustments necessary.
Standard fleet rotation. Twenty-four hours, though we lack a sunset.

topics.visible_label: Is your inventory of office supplies sufficient?
topics.mood_hint: Station logistics guarantees basic desk operational limits.
response_pool: I have half a carton of replacement stylus caps. It will last the quarter.
Standard requisition forms are approved quarterly. Supply levels are adequate.
The terminal stylus is functional. I do not use physical paper, so supply limits are negligible.

topics.visible_label: What is your preferred terminal backup schedule?
topics.mood_hint: Backups are automatically processed by backend protocols.
response_pool: The automated system runs every six hours. Manual backups are redundant.
Redundant cloud storage handles everything automatically. No input required.
The local drive copies files on the hour. It is a completely standard routine.

topics.visible_label: Are you worried about the upcoming quarterly corporate audit?
topics.mood_hint: He detests arbitrary administrative inspections.
response_pool: Worried? No. Annoyed? Extremely. They spend three days checking my cable ties instead of my routing efficiency.
Audit preparation is just endless paperwork. Form 99-C after Form 99-D. It is a complete waste of bandwidth.
The auditors are spreadsheet clerks who have never seen a solar flare. I do not dread their visits, I just dread their questions.

topics.visible_label: How do you get any exercise in a cramped relay station like this?
topics.mood_hint: He hates the physical limitations of his posting.
response_pool: I use a tension band twice a day. It is entirely uninspired, but it prevents muscle atrophy.
There is a small treadmill in deck three, but the bearings are shot. It sounds like a rock crusher.
I pace the length of the command deck. Twelve paces forward, twelve paces back. Truly riveting.

topics.visible_label: Is your twelve-hour monitor shift standard for your rank?
topics.mood_hint: He feels overworked and underappreciated.
response_pool: It is standard for this posting. Higher-tier stations run eight-hour rotations with proper relief officers.
Yes. Though 'twelve hours' often becomes fourteen when the incoming shift operator oversleeps.
Twelve hours of staring of blinking cargo codes. It is a miracle my retinas haven't burned out entirely.

topics.visible_label: Is the hazard pay worth being stuck in deep space?
topics.mood_hint: He considers financial prying highly unprofessional.
response_pool: That is highly personal data. Comms protocol dictates we discuss cargo logistics, not bank scrip.
My compensation is regulated by my officer rank, which is currently... undervalued. Let us leave it at that.
Hazard pay? This is a category-four safe relay. There is no hazard pay. Just standard desk scrip.

topics.visible_label: Is there any way you can speed up your manifest processing times?
topics.mood_hint: He hates having his absolute competence questioned.
response_pool: I route manifests at maximum standard safe speed. If you want haste, expect regulatory errors.
My processing times are in the top ten percent of the sector. The bottleneck is your surface transmitter, not my terminal.
Haste is the enemy of accuracy. I refuse to compromise protocol just because your cargo is rotting.

topics.visible_label: Any improvement on the coffee front?
topics.mood_hint: He has incredibly strong feelings about his morning brew.
response_pool: None. The synthetic blend up here tastes like burnt rubber. I am desperately hunting for a real light roast.
I have submitted three formal complaints about the ration packages. Real beans are impossible to find out here.
Everything is synthetic sludge. If I could get my hands on fresh ground beans, this station would be bearable.
The supply vessel brought nothing but medium-roast instant packets. It is a complete insult to my palate.
Still drinking liquid coal. I am genuinely considering trading my spare boots for a bag of genuine arabica.
No progress. The logistics manager told me coffee quality is non-essential data. The man has no culture.

topics.visible_label: I might try growing some actual coffee beans down in my domes.
topics.mood_hint: This is the absolute way to his heart.
response_pool: That would be outstanding. Keep me posted on that project. Seriously.
Real agricultural beans? If you pull that off, I will personally guarantee your shipping priority.
Now that is an enterprise worth tracking. Let me know the moment you clear a harvest.
A surface-grown light roast? You manage that, and I might just forgive your previous data errors.
If you can cultivate a clean arabica down there, I will make sure your customs clearance is flawless.
That would be a historic achievement for this sector. Please tell me you are being serious.

topics.visible_label: Stop crying about a little bad coffee.
topics.mood_hint: He does not take kindly to people insulting his standards.
response_pool: It is about basic quality of life. I do not expect a surface dweller to understand.
I am not crying. I am maintaining standard requirements for an officer.
I will file that comment right where it belongs. Goodbye.
Crying? It is a matter of neurological focus. Good caffeine yields good data routing.
Your lack of refinement is noted. Let us return to the logistics files.
A highly unhelpful remark. Some of us actually have standards to uphold on this station.

topics.visible_label: What are your ambitions regarding becoming a ship captain?
topics.mood_hint: He loves boasting about his actual qualifications.
response_pool: I passed the GSC-3 Exam. The General Space Command Exam. Scored in the 97th percentile, and they have me routing tomato manifests!
I have filed twelve formal appeals regarding my placement. My GSC-3 Exam scores speak for themselves. The board just chooses not to read them.
I am meant for a bridge, not a relay terminal. I have the training. I have the scores. I just do not have the political connections.
GSC-3 Level certification is no joke. It covers fleet maneuver tactics and deep-space survival strategy. And look at me now.
I should be commanding an Aurelius-class long-range vessel right now. Instead, I am checking seed delivery statuses.
The maritime division is completely blind. A 97th percentile on the GSC-3 Exam used to mean automatic sub-captaincy.

topics.visible_label: I see you are not a captain yet, dork. Haha.
topics.mood_hint: He is absolutely seething.
response_pool: That does it. I have a Complaint Form 77-A with your name on it, pal.
I do not have to sit here and take insults from a dirt farmer. Let us just get to the trade data.
Watch your mouth. I can make your logistics a living nightmare down there.
That is an explicit breach of communication protocol. Do not test my authority to freeze your accounts.
You think you are clever? Let us see how clever you feel when your seed order arrives two weeks late.
Highly hostile tone. I am blacklisting your signal priority for the next twenty-four hours.

topics.visible_label: You are still stuck at a desk instead of a ship bridge?
topics.mood_hint: You are actively making an enemy for life.
response_pool: Highly unprofessional. I am logging this interaction as a Form 77-A violation.
I am an officer in waiting. Keep mocking me and your next cargo markup will reflect it.
Some things are out of my control. The assignment committee will rectify this eventually.
Your humor is completely misplaced. Career stagnation within corporate infrastructure is a serious matter.
Laugh all you want. When I finally get my bridge command, your sector will be the first one I audit.
A completely uncalled-for remark. I operate this relay with total integrity despite my placement.

topics.visible_label: What do you do to pass the time up there?
topics.mood_hint: He relaxes slightly when discussing his routines.
response_pool: I look after a small lunar-bonsai tree. It requires precise trimming with medical shears. It keeps me focused.
I am currently learning to play the Ionuke. It is a compact four-stringed instrument. It passes the quiet hours.
Between shifts, I am mostly taking care of Barnaby. He is a remarkably fat orange cat who likes to sleep on the warm server racks.
I practice standard knot-tying and navigation plotting charts manually. One must keep their operational skills sharp.
I maintain my mini-bonsai. The artificial UV lighting on this deck makes the leaves drop if I am not careful.
I am working through a manual on ancient star-mapping. It is far more stimulating than talking to surface operators.

topics.visible_label: Can you put Barnaby the cat on the viewscreen camera?
topics.mood_hint: He absolutely worships his cat.
response_pool: He is currently sound asleep on the core switch housing. I am not waking him up for you, but he sends his regards.
Barnaby is currently busy ignoring me. He rules this deck, honestly.
Perhaps next cycle. He is currently tracking a rogue maintenance drone down the hall.
He is staring at the wall right now. He is completely orange, so his cognitive processing is somewhat limited.
I just fed him his high-protein gel ration, so he is currently washing himself on top of the main router.
He has assumed his final form: a complete orange ball blocking the primary exhaust fan. He is very content.

topics.visible_label: How is your Ionuke practice coming along?
topics.mood_hint: He likes discussing technical progress, even musical ones.
response_pool: Slowly. The electromagnetic interference from the relay antenna makes the audio pickups buzz constantly.
I have mastered three basic fleet cadences. The four-string layout is logical, though the scale length is tiny.
Barnaby hates the high notes. Whenever I pluck the top string, he tries to swat my fingers off the fretboard.
It is coming along. It is a highly mathematical instrument when you break down the frequency intervals.

topics.visible_label: Your hobbies sound completely ridiculous for a fleet officer.
topics.mood_hint: He will immediately withdraw into his corporate shell.
response_pool: They maintain psychological stability during long-term isolation. It is standard protocol.
I did not ask for your evaluation of my personal life. Let us stick to business.
Barnaby has more discipline than most surface operators I deal with.
A shallow perspective. Bonsai cultivation requires immense patience and tactical foresight.
I find your lack of hobbies explains your sloppy data submittals. Good day.
They are disciplined pastimes. I do not expect a honyocker to grasp the value of structural relaxation.

topics.visible_label: Are any of the other surface farmers making mistakes?
topics.mood_hint: Nothing brings him joy quite like a catastrophic amateur failure.
response_pool: You hear about the Hydro-Lettuce farmer in Sector 18? Guy completely flooded his own domes! Haha. Absolute amateur.
The sector is full of total Honyockers. Someone in Sector 4 managed to vent their entire fertilizer payload into the vacuum last night.
Spacetatoes will grow literally anywhere, yet I don't know how most of these guys manage to completely screw it up.
The guy over in Sector 12 cross-contaminated his water lines with bio-waste. The telemetry logs are hilarious.
Some absolute honyocker in Sector 9 tried to upgrade his harvester drone firmware and wiped the entire logic board.
I am currently watching a dome failure alert in Sector 22. Someone forgot to close a primary airlock latch. Unbelievable.

topics.visible_label: You should have some respect for how hard farming on the moon is.
topics.mood_hint: He has zero sympathy for incompetence.
response_pool: It is basic atmospheric management. It is not rocket science.
If they cannot read a simple chemical balance chart, they should not be holding a lease.
I reserve my respect for people who can follow standard operating procedures.
Hard work does not excuse procedural non-compliance. Read the manual or lose the contract.
It is basic automation. You program the loop, you monitor the parameters. It is simple math.
The system does ninety percent of the labor. All they have to do is not crash the terminal.

topics.visible_label: How many Complaint Form 77-A files have you actually processed today?
topics.mood_hint: He loves using corporate policy as a weapon.
response_pool: Fourteen so far. I am processing a major code red for an absolute honyocker down in Sector 3.
I just finalized an Etiquette & Protocol Non-Compliance Form 77-A on the morning shift supervisor. He was yelling on an open channel.
I have a stack of them ready for regional logistics. People need to learn that actions have regulatory consequences.
I love a fresh Form 77-A file. It is the only way to clean up the garbage on these sub-space frequencies.

topics.visible_label: Keep your forms to yourself, or I will report you for bad service.
topics.mood_hint: He knows the rulebook better than you ever will.
response_pool: Go right ahead. I am fully compliant with Code Section 9. My terminal logs are completely pristine.
You cannot report me for executing standard screening protocols. Try it and see who gets frozen first.
I am the reporting authority for this entire hub sector, pal. Your counter-complaint will land directly in my recycle bin.
Amusing. I am literally auditing your manifest as we speak. I would think twice about filing anything.

topics.visible_label: Have you ever experienced a full artificial gravity failure on the relay?
topics.mood_hint: He likes discussing the technical vulnerabilities of the hub.
response_pool: Once during a solar flare last cycle. The main dampener relays fused. Everything went weightless for three standard hours.
We had a brief failure in deck four's gravitational core last winter. It makes routing logistics quite complex when the consoles float.
The primary reactor had a cold restart. For about forty minutes, we had to wear magnetic boots just to stay anchored.

topics.visible_label: What happens to Barnaby during artificial gravity drops?
topics.mood_hint: He finds the image of his floating cat highly amusing.
response_pool: It is hilarious. Barnaby has zero tracking control in zero gees. He floats like an orange balloon, claws out, deeply offended.
He turns into an orange pufferfish. He drifts around the room, bouncing softly off the servers, glaring at me the entire time.
I have to tether him to his basket using a cargo strap. Otherwise, he floats up into the intake fans. He does not appreciate it.

topics.visible_label: What do you think of the cargo transport pilots docking at the hub?
topics.mood_hint: He holds commercial pilots in very low regard.
response_pool: They are completely undisciplined. Most of them ignore basic docking protocols and drift into the lanes.
They are bus drivers with thrusters. Zero navigational elegance. They just punch the autopilot and hope for the best.
I spend half my shift yelling at freighter pilots who cannot align their docking clamps on the first pass.

topics.visible_label: Do the pilots have any respect for the LC-4 Relay staff?
topics.mood_hint: He feels deeply underappreciated by the transit crews.
response_pool: None. They treat us like automated beacons. They do not realize we coordinate ninety percent of their transit logs.
They call us desk operators. Meanwhile, we are the ones preventing them from colliding with bulk cargo platforms.
I have filed three separate Form 77-A complaints on commercial crews this week. They lack basic fleet etiquette.

hostile: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. State your situation.
This is Kovacs. Go for LC-4 Relay. Make it quick.
Kovacs here. I am recording this channel. State your business.
LC-4 Relay. I am in no mood for protocol breaches today. State your query.
Kovacs audio-clear. Keep this brief, my terminal is flooding with errors.
sour: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. What do you need?
Kovacs here. I am in the middle of a diagnostic loop.
LC-4 Relay. Standing by for trade data payload.
LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. Let's get 'er moving.
Specialist Kovacs monitoring. Please present your shipping IDs.
neutral: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. Go ahead.
This is Kovacs, go for LC-4 Relay.
Kovacs here. Standing by for surface manifest.
LC-4 Relay standing by. State your trade parameters.
Kovacs audio-active. Proceed with your standard transmission.
happy: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. Good timing, actually.
Kovacs here. The data lines are perfectly clear today.
LC-4 Relay. You caught me in a reasonable mood.
Kovacs here. Main routing matrix is clear, let us get your orders in.
LC-4 Echo-Alpha, Kovacs clear. How is life on the surface today?
elated: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. Always good to hear from you.
Kovacs here. Surprisingly decent day up on the hub. What can I do for you?
LC-4 Relay. Comms are optimal. Ready when you are, friend.
Kovacs here! Barnaby is behaving and my routing queue is empty. Let us do some business.
LC-4 Relay audio-clear. Excellent timing, whatcha got for me?.

idle_transmissions: Station Broadcast: Week {week} regional agricultural reports are due. Double-check your values.
Kovacs personal log — open channel warning: GSC-3 Exam certificates remain valid for three standard cycles.
LC-4 Technical Alert: Minor data packet loss detected in Sector 12. Honyockers probably tripped over a cable.
Corporate Memo: Etiquette & Protocol Non-Compliance Form 77-A filings have increased by twelve percent this quarter.
Kovacs here. Just for the record, Barnaby has caught zero mice, but his server warming routine is flawless.
Weather Advisory: Solar radiation spike expected. Ensure dome shield integrity is logged.
LC-4 Relay Note: The outer rim shipping lanes are clear tonight. Wish I was out there charting them.
Kovacs: To the operator who submitted a corrupt shipping manifest: I am officially processing a Form 77-A code red.
Logistics Alert: Cargo backup reported at primary docking ring. Ensure all transit crates are labeled accurately.
Kovacs: If another person asks me to manually check their seed order pool, I am shutting off my audio receiver.
Station Chatter: The cafeteria paste machine has been recalibrated. Joy is officially down another five percent.
Kovacs personal note: The lunar-bonsai tree just cleared two centimeters of new growth. Excellent density parameters.
System Advisory: Automatic billing scripts running for Week {week}. Check your Star-Scrip accounts immediately.
